body.title: [sprint] Router: explicit-model path requires a sanctioned pin_reason, else reject + system_alarm
body.description:
**Problem:** once all production callers route by domain (T-inference-migrate-pinners), the explicit-model path must be CLOSED to anything but the 3 sanctioned uses, or it silently reopens as an escape hatch that defeats 'cheapest every time' (CP6 — no escape hatches). **Proposed shape:** add `pin_reason` to the request (shim.py) — one of {inference_test, experiment, model_competition}; device.py's explicit-model branch requires a recognized pin_reason. CHOSEN DEFAULT (mine, CP6-aligned, not attributed to Akien): a pinned model with NO sanctioned pin_reason is REJECTED with a clear error + system_alarm + loud log — never silently rerouted (silent reroute would hide a caller doing the wrong thing). Unpinned requests route by {domain, urgency} as normal. **Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/shim.py (pin_reason field, if not already added by the migrate ticket), devices/inference/device.py (explicit-model branch gate + system_alarm + logging). **Design rules:** NO SQLITE; log the routing/trust decision at the crossing; CP6 — a pin must name its sanctioned purpose. **Scope boundary:** IN: pin_reason gate + reject + system_alarm + logging on the explicit-model path. OUT: the migration itself (gated before this), domain routing (T-inference-domain-tag). **Test plan (stub-first red->green):** (1) a pin with pin_reason=model_competition dispatches to that exact model; (2) a pin with NO pin_reason is REJECTED (raises + system_alarm), does not dispatch to the pinned model; (3) an unpinned request routes via the selector by domain; (4) the crossing-log names the pin purpose on sanctioned pins and the rejection on unsanctioned ones. **Completion criteria:** explicit-model dispatch requires a sanctioned pin_reason; unsanctioned pin rejected + system_alarm + logged; unpinned always routes by domain; tests green.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.intention: I intend that model-pinning is impossible except for the three sanctioned uses (inference-test, experiment, model-competition), so the cost-optimizing router can never be silently bypassed.
